title: __Vtrigcurrexpr_* not declared in scope across split output files
body:
After upgrading to Verilator 5.036, compilation fails when Verilator emits large modules across multiple `.cpp` files. It seems that some internal variables like `__Vtrigcurrexpr_h1cad7197__0` are defined only in one file but are referenced in another, without declaration.

Here's the error:

```
ver___024root__DepSet_h96c49fed__23.cpp: In function 'void ver___024root___eval_triggers__act__14(ver___024root*)':
ver___024root__DepSet_h96c49fed__23.cpp:4753:46: error: '__Vtrigcurrexpr_h1cad7197__0' was not declared in this scope
 4753 |     vlSelfRef.__Vtrigprevexpr_h1cad7197__0 = __Vtrigcurrexpr_h1cad7197__0;
      |                                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

This seems to happen only when Verilator decides to split the generated C++ into multiple files. I'm not sure if there's an easy way to reproduce it with a small design, since it appears related to optimization behavior on larger inputs. After bisecting, I found that the issue starts happening with PR #5798. 

## Version

Verilator 5.036 (2025-04-27)
Ubuntu 22.04
code_blocks:
ver___024root__DepSet_h96c49fed__23.cpp: In function 'void ver___024root___eval_triggers__act__14(ver___024root*)':
ver___024root__DepSet_h96c49fed__23.cpp:4753:46: error: '__Vtrigcurrexpr_h1cad7197__0' was not declared in this scope
 4753 |     vlSelfRef.__Vtrigprevexpr_h1cad7197__0 = __Vtrigcurrexpr_h1cad7197__0;
      |                                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
